In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]

In 2006, Google moved into about 300,000 square feet (27,900 m2) of office space at 111 Eighth Avenue in Manhattan, New York City. The office houses its largest advertising sales team.[294] In 2010, Google bought the building housing the headquarters, in a deal that valued the property at around $1.9 billion.[295][296] In March 2018, Google's parent company Alphabet bought the nearby Chelsea Market building for $2.4 billion. The sale is touted as one of the most expensive real estate transactions for a single building in the history of New York.[297][298][299][300] In November 2018, Google announced its plan to expand its New York City office to a capacity of 12,000 employees.[301] The same December, it was announced that a $1 billion, 1,700,000-square-foot (160,000 m2) headquarters for Google would be built in Manhattan's Hudson Square neighborhood.[302][303] Called Google Hudson Square, the new campus is projected to more than double the number of Google employees working in New York City.[304]

By late 2006, Google established a new headquarters for its AdWords division in Ann Arbor, Michigan.[305] In November 2006, Google opened offices on Carnegie Mellon's campus in Pittsburgh, focusing on shopping-related advertisement coding and smartphone applications and programs.[306][307] Other office locations in the U.S. include Atlanta; Austin; Boulder, Colorado; Cambridge, Massachusetts; San Francisco; Seattle and Kirkland, Washington; Birmingham, Michigan; Reston, Virginia, Washington, D.C.,[308] and Madison, Wisconsin.[309]
